key_points: 186 ballistic missiles fired at UAE (172 intercepted, 1 landed on territory, 13 fell into sea); 812 drones (755 intercepted, 57 struck within country)
3 foreign nationals killed (Pakistani, Nepali, Bangladeshi); 58-70 injured across multiple nationalities
DXB Terminal 3 struck ~01:30 Mar 1; 4 staff injured. Fairmont Hotel Palm Jumeirah hit by drone (major fire). Burj Al Arab facade damaged by interception fragments.
US Consulate Dubai struck Mar 3 — fire in parking lot and chancellery; all personnel accounted for
Jebel Ali Port fire from debris; DP World briefly suspended then resumed. AWS data center fire caused cloud-service disruptions.
Al Dhafra Air Base and Al Minhad Air Base (RAF/Australian forces) both targeted
Interceptor depletion projected ~7 days at current consumption rate. UAE has THAAD, Patriot PAC-3, Pantsir-S1, Cheongung II, Skynight.
~20,000 passengers stranded; 80%+ of Dubai flights cancelled. Emirates suspended until 23:59 Mar 4. EASA advised European carriers to avoid UAE airspace.
Panic buying Mar 1 widespread; shelves restocked by Mar 3. Price controls imposed by Ministry of Economy.
US State Dept Level 3 Reconsider Travel; non-emergency staff ordered depart Mar 2; DEPART NOW advisory Mar 3

situation_assessment.assessment: HIGH. The UAE continues to face periodic Iranian missile and drone attacks, primarily targeting US and coalition military infrastructure such as Al Dhafra Air Base. Air defense systems are intercepting the majority of inbound threats, though several projectiles and debris impacts have caused casualties and infrastructure damage.

STABLE TO INCREASING. Iranian launch tempo has slowed overall but remains capable of periodic strikes. If the conflict continues, interceptor stockpiles across Gulf states will gradually be consumed, increasing the probability of successful missile impacts.
Presence of US military facilities, proximity to major regional logistics hubs, continued Iranian retaliation against coalition partners, and potential proxy escalation in the region.
Continued interceptions of missiles and drones across the UAE. Aviation disruption continues with reduced flight operations and airspace management restrictions.
Continued missile and drone interceptions are likely. Aviation disruptions will persist and may worsen if additional strikes occur near airport infrastructure.
Sustained intermittent missile threats likely. If interceptor inventories decline or Iranian proxies activate additional fronts, risk to infrastructure and civilian areas could increase.

DXB: OPEN – Partial operations. DWC: OBSTRUCTED. AUH: OPEN – Partial operations. SHJ: OPEN – Partial operations. Flight schedules remain unstable with significant cancellations and repatriation flights.

airports.name: Dubai International
airports.notes: Terminal 3 arrival hall offline; limited departures via Concourse A only

airports.name: Al Maktoum Intl (DWC)
airports.notes: Primary diversion field; cargo apron crater patched, runway checks ongoing

airports.name: Abu Dhabi International
airports.notes: Handling DXB diverts; priority for gov/mil sorties

airports.name: Sharjah International
airports.notes: Night ops limited after nearby rocket barrage

borders.name: Ghuwaifat / Al Batha (KSA)
borders.notes: Commercial convoys stacking 14km; priority lane reserved for SAM reloads

borders.name: Hatta / Al Wajajah (Oman)
borders.notes: Used for relief supplies and medevac shuttles

domestic_unrest.notes: Panic buying, school closures, stranded travelers. No protests or mass exodus. Govt messaging reassuring market stability.

energy_infra.notes: Drone strike on Jebel Ali Port — berth fire from debris. ADNOC facilities under threat. Jebel Ali power station complex struck (includes desalination capability).

evac_routes.label: Dubai → Muscat
evac_routes.notes: MCT operating normally as of 03 MAR. Only Gulf FIR currently open to commercial traffic.

evac_routes.label: Dubai → Riyadh
evac_routes.notes: Riyadh under direct rocket attack as of 03 MAR 16:15Z. Route follows 250km Gulf coast road adjacent to active energy strike targets.

water_infra.notes: Jebel Ali power station complex (desalination-linked) struck. Experts warn of uninhabitable conditions if grid and water infra severely damaged. No confirmed water supply disruption yet.

iw_matrix.notes: DXB/AUH/DWC/SHJ: obstructed. Evacuation flights resumed on limited basis. ~20,000 passengers stranded.
186 BMs fired cumulative. UAE MoD: 11 BMs + 123 drones intercepted Mar 3 alone. Intercept rate 92.5%.
812 drones fired cumulative; 755 intercepted (93%). Jebel Ali port, Dubai consulate, AUH perimeter struck.
Jebel Ali Port berth fire. Jebel Ali power station struck (desalination-linked). ADNOC under threat.
Jebel Ali power station complex struck. No confirmed water supply disruption. Experts warn uninhabitable conditions if infra severely damaged.
Panic buying, school closures, stranded travelers. No protests or mass exodus.
Dubai US Consulate struck Mar 3 19:30Z — contained fire, no injuries. US personnel ordered to depart.
Bloomberg depletion window Mar 7–10. US resupply reportedly blocked. Intercept rate holding at 92–93%.
